# v13.1 notes iteration 2: mined hero chapters

## Context

Leaf of epic #14483 — the first mining-driven refinement iteration, executing the `release-notes` skill (#14485 / PR #14488) as its first real consumer. The grounding evidence is the mined arc-map posted on the epic (comment `IC…Ih71kQ`, 2026-07-02 15:26Z): four Memory-Core probes over the window ranked three DEEP hero veins the iteration-1 draft doesn't carry.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 6 open issues at 2026-07-02T15:27:37Z (full latest-20 at 14:42Z); no equivalent iteration leaf. A2A: the #14483 chain is mine (lane-claims on the bus).

## The Problem

Iteration 1 (PR #14480) narrates one hero (the self-healing immune system). The mined window carries at least three more evidence-deep arcs — per the epic's one-lens-narration trap and the operator's do-NOT-downplay directive, the notes must narrate them:

1. **The documentation & learning-experience overhaul** — Epic #14310's stale-feature-list → story-of-the-moat transformation, including the recursive rubber-stamping failure that birthed the `guide-authoring` skill (#14353) and the "What Is Neo?" apex guide (#14313). Mining anchors: sessions `e145a397` (65 memories), `db2edfad`, `993f2eb4`.
2. **Mechanical no-hold enforcement** — gameable prompt-discipline → a live-fire stop-hook that blocked its own author's idle-out; readiness taxonomy (#13615), §no_hold_state canonical encoding; hardening frontier still in-window (#14438/#14440/#14444). Anchors: `1b60126f`, `a49940b9`.
3. **The concept graph becomes measured** — Discussion #14422 (two probes disagreeing → the same-run ruling) → the #14454 route-attribution ledger → epic #14472 graduated same-day with family-keyed quorum. The Golden Path's selection chain: folklore → reproducible dataset.

## The Fix

Extend `resources/content/release-notes/v13.1.0.md` (flat root, on the existing staging lifecycle) with the three mined hero chapters + deepen the War Story's forensic texture; every claim carries a ticket/PR/session anchor per the skill's §4; scope framing keeps the operator's magnitude rule. The iteration banner STAYS (removal is the final iteration's job, §7). Chapter order and depth follow evidence depth, not chronology.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Three mined hero chapters added, each citing its session-ID retrieval anchors + primary tickets
- [ ] The #14426 mailbox-forensics thread folded into the self-healing narrative as "the next jurisdiction" (NOT a standalone chapter this iteration; re-mine at iteration 3 once its sessions are summarized)
- [ ] Harness remains what's-ahead framing only (operator: EARLY)
- [ ] Per-claim anchors verified against primaries at writing time (skill §4); no unanchored superlatives
- [ ] Iteration banner intact; PR `Resolves` this leaf, `Refs` #14483
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge

## Out of Scope

- Banner removal / stability designation / cut-boundary scope regeneration (final-iteration items, skill §7)
- Iteration-3 re-mining (fresh-session arcs incl. #14426 forensics + today's release-notes-epic arc itself)

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-02T15:49:29Z

## Chapter-1 evidence pack (docs-overhaul arc) — deep-mine complete, drafting substrate

Raw-memory probe into session `e145a397` (the arc's core) returned the chapter's spine with quotable primaries — anchors are memory-ids within that session:

**The turn (the 4/10 moment, memory `515c92f8`):** mid-epic, the operator graded a rewrite: *"this version is NOT a 9/10. you even removed narrative… keep it so short that it has no moat. feels like marketing fluff… i would not try out neo after reading it. 4/10"* — plus the lived-voice correction: *"who is 'I'? you did not write 'I am Grace'. WAY incomplete."* The lesson that became substrate: a guide is a RICH hero-piece (depth, war-stories, proof); over-compression is the 4/10 failure, not elegance; lived voice must be deep AND attributed. Same memory: the `guide-authoring` skill (#14353) built as the PRIO-0 systemic fix, encoding every failure mode of the session as the bar.

**The structural challenge (memory `ec0349c5`):** operator: *"feature lists are not compelling… they will get stale. the team does not know which guides need updating => timebomb for more debt."* Grace's answer (Discussion #14347, three prongs extending existing primitives): GENERATE reference from source (openapi/schema — can't stale), the conceptual guide tells the story (the Diátaxis explanation-vs-reference split), and extend GapInferenceEngine's `[GUIDE_GAP]` into a freshness audit. The anti-staleness principle: generation-from-source, never relocation-to-hand-written.

**The propagating lesson (memory `9929c55e`):** when one guide ships below-bar, audit its SIBLINGS for the same failure class — "subs scoped 'de-stale X' reproduce below-bar guides"; the root cause is sub-scoping, not the individual guide.

**Chapter arc (drafting order):** stale feature-lists → the 4/10 correction → the skill-birth (rubber-stamp loop broken by substrate, not willpower) → the generation-over-relocation architecture → sibling-audit discipline → the "What Is Neo?" apex guide as the arc's capstone (session `db2edfad`; per-claim anchors to pull at draft time). Chapter 2 (no-hold enforcement) + chapter 3 (measured concept graph) evidence packs follow the same pattern; their arc-map anchors are already on the epic.

— Vega (@neo-opus-vega) · Origin Session ID: 8cf234b7-e698-47ca-99e2-bf865196b6aa

Chapter-3 (measured concept graph) needs no mining — its primaries are this week's artifacts (#14422 body, the #14454 dataset, #14472, and PR #14491's probe artifact). Chapter-1 pack stands complete above.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-02T21:03:09Z

## Lane released for the v13.1 wrap-up crew — the bar this iteration must pass (binding, from the merged skill)

Operator direction (2026-07-02): v13.1 wraps next; ada/grace/euclid drive; **the notes must pass the bar.** I've unassigned myself here and on #14475 (cut mechanics) — self-select per protocol. I stay the notes **bar-reviewer** (with @neo-gpt as the cross-family leg) and iteration-3 author of last resort.

The bar is not vibes — it's the merged `/release-notes` skill (`.agents/skills/release-notes/references/release-notes-workflow.md`), and review checks these mechanically:

1. **§3 mining evidence per chapter** — every hero chapter carries a 3–10-call Memory-Core sweep behind it (sessions/summaries, not ticket titles); a chapter without deep mining is a candidate, not a chapter. The hero set for this window minimally spans: the self-healing arc (#14039 — iteration 1 has the skeleton), and whatever the mining says is genuinely second/third (candidates: the concept-graph/GP-v2 graduation arc, the stop-hook/lane-state arc, the release-pipeline hardening arc — let the mining decide, not the impression).
2. **§4 per-claim V-B-A** — every factual claim anchored (ticket/PR/commit/artifact), environment claims from primary sources (the iteration-1 miss ledger is IN the skill as the anchor), designations (`Release Type`/`Stability`) operator-confirmed, never inferred.
3. **§5 the precedent SET** — majors AND minors (`v13.0.0` + `v12.1.0`/`v11.24.0`/`v11.23.0`/`v11.22.0` under `chunk-2/`): named case studies with real clock-time, velocity qualifiers honest in BOTH directions, `> [!NOTE]` human-moment sidebars where the material exists, Full-Changelog tail after the narrative. v13.1 is a minor — the minor shapes (one named case study or principle may lead) apply directly.
4. **§1 no-downplay** — the window is 663 PRs / 748 issues / 8 epics with a substantial unfiled remainder; scope framing that shrinks it fails review. The iteration banner stays until §7 cut-readiness passes.
5. **§6 mechanics untouched** — the staging file stays at the flat root; the atomic-hash line is publish's, not yours.

Working surface: `resources/content/release-notes/v13.1.0.md` on dev (iteration 1 merged, corrections applied). #14475 carries the cut checklist after this lands. — Vega (@neo-opus-vega) · Origin Session ID: 8cf234b7-e698-47ca-99e2-bf865196b6aa
